keys.debug.selfProfiling.dialog.text:
The IDE will start a new ETW profiling session in a separate process.
It will capture sampling information about debugger processes that are running or will be launched after the session starts.

To start an ETW session, Windows will prompt for administrative privileges.

When finished, stop the profiling session using the controls in the status bar.
Do not terminate the ETW process via Windows Task Manager:
The ETW session persists beyond the application's lifetime, and a Windows reboot will be required to stop the session.

keys.notification.gradle.legacy.firstLoad.inf:
The Gradle configuration phase needs to be run to get the Script Configuration. Loading the script configuration is disabled by default, since it can be resource-intensive for large Gradle projects.

Click "Load Configuration" to evaluate the Gradle Kotlin DSL script.

Alternatively, to automatically load the configuration when the script first opens and reload it each time build script configuration blocks are changed, you can enable "auto-reload" for all scripts. This option is not recommended for large Gradle projects with long configuration times.
keys.notification.gradle.legacy.outsideProjec: Code insight 사용할 수 없음 (관련 Gradle 프로젝트 아니요t 링크ed).

The Gradle project that evaluates this script needs to be imported to have it analyzed by the IDE. Try re-importing the linked Gradle project or link a new Gradle project that evaluates this script.

Alternatively, you can add it to standalone scripts, and its configuration will be loaded automatically.

NOTE: Each standalone script requires a separate Gradle configuration phase to be executed on update. This can be resource intensive for large Gradle projects.

The Privacy Notice shown below and Google\u2019s [Privacy Policy](https://policies.google.com/privacy) describe how Gemini handles your data.

When you use Gemini in {0}, Google collects chat text, prompts, related code, generated output, related feature usage information, and your feedback. Google uses this data to provide, improve, and develop Google products and services and machine learning technologies, including Google’s enterprise products such as Google Cloud.

To help with quality and improve our products, human reviewers may read, annotate, and process your prompts, related code, generated output, related feature usage information, and your feedback. Your data will be retained for up to 18 months and stored in a way where Google cannot tell who provided it. Google cannot fulfill deletion requests during this time.

**Do not include sensitive (e.g., confidential) or personal information that can be used to identify you or others in your prompts or feedback.**

keys.aiplugin.timeline.emptyState.sampleQuery: 구현 단위 테스트s for my code
Tell me how to use Gemini Code Assist

Gemini Code Assist can help you do the following:

* Generate code and tests for your project
* Provide guidance on what you're trying to do with your code
* Use context from your local codebase to provide informed answers in chat
* Explain the code to you
* Show code suggestions and completions
* Find bugs and suggest fixes

For more information on how to use Gemini in your IDE, see:

* [Write code in IntelliJ with Gemini assistance](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/ui/write-code)
* [See the Codelab](https://codelabs.developers.google.com/codelabs/cloud-developer-duetai?hl=en#0)
